titleStmt.title: ʿAdwā, ʾƎndā ʾAbbā Garimā, ʾAbbā Garimā I
ʾAbbā Garimā Gospels I

editionStmt.p: The images of the manuscript used here are the microfilm photographs collected by Donald M Davies (and described by William Macomber).

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

supportDesc.foliation: The description follows the foliation provided by Macomber (before the restoration).
supportDesc.condition:
Consists of leaves from five different manuscripts. Areas of poor legibility are numerous.
The manuscript was in very poor condition, folia in disorder and many loose, margins missing. It was significantly restored during the recent restoration effort: "The front board was removed by untying threads that were wrapped around the hinge rod. The spine vellum pieces, which were sewn into the first pages, were unthreaded where possible, releasing them, and creating access to the spine folds. The first twelve pages - illuminations and text - were detached and the repairs running across some of the pages were unthreaded.
The torn and damaged edges were repaired in small areas with laminations of Taizan 36gsm smooth toned Japanese paper using a parchment size adhesive.
I had taken with me a variety of Japanese papers - different weights, tones, textures, and a selection of differing thicknesses and tones of vellum. Although I had seen photographs of some pages it was difficult to be sure which material would be most suitable until I actually confronted the manuscript. The Taizan was a sympathetic match.
It would have been inappropriate to build up the large missing areas with new vellum as the stresses created would have transferred through to the original vellum causing more possible damage. Also the new pieces of vellum would have been largely unsupported by the rest of the volume and would therefore be vulnerable to damage, again possibly transferring through to the original. The torn edges and small vulnerable areas were consolidated with the toned Japanese paper.
Two laminations of Taizan paper were profiled to the spine edge shape of each single folio, edge pared and attached to the vellum page. No single folios were guarded together to create a double folio, the validity of which I could not be certain of.
A loose vellum guard was added around the outside of the spine folds to strengthen and support the sewing.
The loose text pages were repaired similarly and reassembled in their correct order. New sewing hemp cord was connected into the existing sewing of the text block, and the newly arranged and repaired folios were sewn in using the normal Coptic sewing method which had been employed throughout the rest of the book.
A blank, toned vellum flyleaf was sewn in to protect the first page from the inside of the copper board.
The three later vellum spine pieces were sewn into the stub of the new flyleaf enabling freer opening, and not hindering the opening of the first few pages.
The front board was reattached using the threads from the sewing which were wrapped and tied around the hinge rod securely." (Extreme Bookbinding – a fascinating preservation project in EthiopiaSkin DeepLesterCapon2008262-11http://www.hewit.com/skin_deep/?volume=26&article=1)

origin.origDate: The main codicological unit has been tentatively dated on the basis of radiocarbon datation to 530-660 CE by Jacques Mercier

bibl.title: Catalogue of Ethiopian Manuscripts from Abbā Garimā, As̆atan (Church of St. Mary), Axum (Church of Zion), Dabra Bizan, Dabra Dāmo, Dabra Libanos, Gunda Gundē, Kebrān, Lālibalā (Church of the Savior of the World, Emmanuel Church), Maqalē, Ura, Kidāna Meḥrat, Monastery of Dabra Dāgā (Church of St. Stephen), Monastery of Lake Zewāy, Dabra Māryām, National Library and Haiq from Microfilms in the Collection of Dr. Donald Davies, De Land, Florida and Godfrey, Ontario, and of the Hill Monastic Manuscript Library, St. John's University, Collegeville, Minnesota
New Studies of the Structure and the Texts of Abba Garima Ethiopian Gospels
Afriques
The Garima Gospels: Early Illuminated Gospel Books from Ethiopia
Manar al-Athar Monograph
L'évangéliaire éthiopien du couvent d'Abba Garima et ses attaches avec l'ancien art chrétien de Syrie
Cahiers Archéologiques
Un nouvel évangéliaire éthiopien illustré du monastère d’Abba Garima
Synthronon. Art et archéologie de la fin de l’Antiquité et du Moyen Age. Recueil d’études par André Grabar et un groupe de ses disciples
Bibliothèque des Cahiers Archéologiques
The "True Story" of the Abba Gärima Gospels
Comparative Oriental Manuscript Studies Newsletter

Review of Judith S. McKenzie and Francis Watson, <hi rend="italics">The Garima Gospels: Early Illuminated Gospel Books from Ethiopia</hi>, with Preface and Photographs by Michael Gervers and Contributions by Matthew R. Crawford, Linda R. Macaulay, Sarah S. Norodom, Andres T. Reyes, and Miranda E. Williams, Manar al-Athar Monograph, 3 (Oxford: Manar al­-Athar, 2016)
Aethiopica
The Textual Background of the Gospel of Matthew in Geʿez
Aethiopica
The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica
La peinture éthiopienne à l’époque axoumite et au XVIII<hi rend="sup">e</hi> siècle
Académie des Inscriptions et Belles­Lettres, Comptes rendus des séances
African Zion: The Sacred Art of Ethiopia

msItem.note: The Ammonian sections with the canons which refer to them are indicated in the margins and the chapters at the top or bottom of the columns.

msItem.title: Wangel za-Mātewos
msItem.note: Lacuna: List of the “tituli” for the Gospel of Matthew: 68 titles

msItem.title: List of the “tituli” for the Gospel of Mark: 48 titles
List of the “tituli” for the Gospel of Luke: 83 titles
List of the “tituli” for the Gospel of John: 18 titles

handNote.desc: The fine hand was dated to the 10th century by Macomber; the unit itself was dated by art historical and radiocarbon analysis to 6th-7th century

item.desc: The Ammonian sections with the canons which refer to them are indicated in the margins and the chapters at the top or bottom of the columns.

msContents.summary: The Four Gospels were originally part of the same manuscript as Abba Garima 2, unit 1

msItem.title: The conclusion of the treatise, Synoptic Presentation concerning the Agreement of Texts of the Four Gospels
List of the “tituli” for the Gospel of John: 18 titles

supportDesc.foliation: The description follows the foliation provided by Macomber (before the restoration).

handNote.desc: 15th or 16th century hand of additio 2 and 3

Difficult to date, but is surely more recent than the date of the manuscript from which the leaf is taken according to Macomber. Getatchew Haile in The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima GospelsAethiopicaGetatchew Haile2016197–26bm:Getatchew2016AGbm:Getatchew2016AG suggested that the note may be original and contemporary with ʾArmāḥ, thus of the 7th century.

item.desc: Record of a donation of lands to the Monastery of ʾAbbā Garimā by King Gabra Masqal, copied in a 17th century hand.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Record of a donation of religious objects to the monastery by King [Sayfa] ʾĀrʿad (1344-1372) ().

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: List of the dates on which different Kings of Ethiopia were to be commemorated and what was to be done on such occasions (, continuing from the previous addition).

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Admonition against damaging or stealing land or property of nǝbura ʾǝd Kǝfla Taʿos and his offspring.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Record of a donation of land to the monastery by a certain Taklo, during the tenure of the abbot Yamāna Krǝstos, by a 17th-18th century hand.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Record of the building of certain churches subject to the monastery by a certain King ʾArmāḥo, possibly to be identified with the King ʾArmāḥ, who lived at the time of Muhammad and who is reported to have become a Muslim (600-640). ().

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Donation of land to the monastery by Daǧǧāzmač Kǝfla ʾIya[ sus] in the 2nd year of King Takla Haymānot (= 1707-1708 A.D., ).

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Record of a donation by a certain Zaʾamānuʾel, copied in a late 15th century hand .

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

msContents.summary: The Four Gospels were originally part of the same manuscript as Abba Garima 2, unit 2

handNote.desc: The hand is probably contemporary to .

decoNote.desc: Circular design that must have followed the Gospel of Luke.
Miniature of St. John the Evangelist (not actually identified, but his is the only miniature missing in Reel 3).

item.desc: Record of the construction of the Monastery of ʾAbbā Garimā by King Gabra Masqal, together with the donation of lands and privileges, copied in a 14th or 15th century hand.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Record of a visit to the monastery by King Baʾǝda Māryām (r. 1468-1478) and of his benefactions on the occasion, copied in a contemporary hand.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Request to remember a certain Zenā Ḥawāryāt and his wife.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Record of a benefaction by King Sarṣa Dǝngǝl (1563-1597), who restored the monastery and gave lands and farming equipment.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

item.desc: Record of a benefaction by officials of Ṭāfā, in a 19th-century hand.

bibl.title: The Marginal Notes in the <hi rend="italics">Abba</hi> Gärima Gospels
Aethiopica

msItem.title: End of the list of chapters of the Gospel of Matthew, chapters 60 to 68

msContents.summary: Leaf from an Old Testament manuscript, probably part of the same manuscript as Abba Garima 2, unit 5

msItem.title: Old Testament
msItem.note: Two leaves have been filmed in Reel 3 (No item: AG00002#p5) that may derive from the same manuscript. They contain Isaiah 30,19-33 and a fragment of the Ascension of Isaiah.

handNote.desc: The hand is similar to and .

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

relation.name: skos:exactMatch
relation.desc: The MS ʾAbbā Garimā 2 is distributed between two volumes

relation.name: skos:exactMatch
relation.desc: The MS ʾAbbā Garimā 3 is distributed between two volumes

relation.name: skos:exactMatch
relation.desc: The Old Testament MS has two leaves within ʾAbbā Garimā 2 and one leaf bound with ʾAbbā Garimā 1
